rulings.Case Title: VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III, LLC, A et al VS. MATTHEW E. CRISP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-07-468736 WITH CASE CGC-05-444880
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFFS VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III, L.L.C., VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY II, L.L.C., JOANNA REES-GALLANTER, VENTURE STRATEGY GROUP L.L.C., D.B.A. V.S.P. CAPITAL, VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III, L.L.C. AND JOHN HAMM'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-07-468736 WITH CASE CGC-05-444880 IS MOOT BASED ON THE COURT'S RULING ON THE DEMURRER IN CASE NUMBER 07-468736. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD O DIAL VS. WILLIS INSURANCE SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Willis Group Holdings Limited'S Motion To Quash Purported Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT WILLIS GROUP HOLDING LTD.'S MOTION TO QUASH PURPORTED SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 14, 2008 PER STIPULATION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JANICE L. YANCEY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT AN OFFICER, DIRECTOR OR MANAGING AGENT ACTED WITH MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD. NEITHER INDUSTRIAL INDEMNITY PETITION NOR MR. HOLLER'S KNOWLEDGE ARE A SUFFICIENT BASIS TO IMPART KNOWLEDGE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS C, D, F, G, H, K, M AND N APPEAR WELL TAKEN. THE 19TH AND 20TH CAUSES OF ACTION ALSO ARE DISMISSED PER PLAINTIFF'S STIPULATION TO DO SO. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH DRURY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Noice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOSEPH MANNERY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Request For Judicial Notice) IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO RELY UPON PROVISIONS OTHER THAN TRAFFIC CODE SECTION 170.2-A, SHE MUST ALLEGE THEM IN THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF ALSO MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT DEMONSTRATES THAT THE CONDUCT COMPLAINED OF IS NOT SUBJECT TO THE IMMUNITY UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 818.8. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: SUE WALDEN VS. GERALD L. FRANCKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify To The Court Of Limited Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT GAROLD FRANCKE'S Motion To Reclassify To The Court Of Limited Jurisdiction IS DENIED. FAILURE TO MEET THE EVIDENTIARY BURDEN OF WALKER V SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 53 CAL. 3D 257, 269 - 270. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: COLLEEN BARGER-CAREY VS. REPUBLIC INDEMNITY COMPANY OF AMERICA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plts' Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT REPUBLIC INDEMNITY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S Motion To Strike PLAINTIFFS' Complaint IS DENIED. THE EXEMPTION UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 425.17 IS APPLICABLE TO THIS MOTION. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS THE ALLEGED FAILURE OF DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE THE BENEFITS OF THE INSURANCE POLICY PURCHASED BY THE EMPLOYER AND IS BASED ON THE TERMS OF THE POLICY AND STATEMENTS MADE IN SELLING THE POLICY TO THE EMPLOYER. WHATEVER POSITIONS DEFENDANT TOOK IN THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION PROCEEDINGS ARE INCIDENTAL TO THE THRUST OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. TO ACCEPT DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT WOULD SUBJECT VIRTUALLY EVERY COVERAGE DISPUTE TO AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. C.C.P. SECTION 425.17 REFLECTS THE LEGISLATURE'S INTENT NOT TO DO SO. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: AARON S. GORFEIN VS. DANIEL RAY BACON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 17. DEFENDANT DANIEL BACON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION ON EACH OF THE CLAIMS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: AMIN MERCHANT AND APEX TRADING HOUSE VS. CHARLES SCHWAB & CO, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of X-Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MARCH 18, 2008 LINE 12. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS CHARLES SCHWAB & CO, PRUDENTIAL EQUITY GROUP AND EDWARD SO'S CROSS-Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award IS GRANTED TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO PRESENT SUFFICIENT PROBATIVE EVIDENCE TO VACATE THE AWARD. THE RECORD REFLECTS THAT PETITIONER WAS GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO CONTINUE THE HEARING AND DECLINED TO DO AND THE RECORD DOES NOT REFLECT AN AGREEMENT TO PRODUCE WITNESSES OR DOCUMENTS. =(302/PJM/PB)
